mission.title: Install and Configure Holmesgpt on Kubernetes
mission.description: HolmesGPT is an open-source AI agent designed for investigating production incidents and identifying root causes. As a CNCF Sandbox project, it integrates with various observability tools to enhance incident response capabilities.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the HolmesGPT Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration using the following command:
```bash
helm repo add holmes https://holmesgpt.dev/helm
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install HolmesGPT
steps.description:
Install HolmesGPT in your Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and create a namespace:
```bash
helm install holmes holmes/holmes --version 0.20.0 --namespace holmes --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the HolmesGPT pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace holmes
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Update the resource limits for the HolmesGPT deployment by editing the values file or using the following command:
```bash
helm upgrade holmes holmes/holmes --namespace holmes --set resources.requests.cpu=100m --set resources.requests.memory=2048Mi --set resources.limits.memory=2048Mi
```

steps.title: Set up monitoring
steps.description:
Enable monitoring by configuring the Prometheus toolset in HolmesGPT:
```bash
helm upgrade holmes holmes/holmes --namespace holmes --set toolsets.prometheus/metrics.enabled=true
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of HolmesGPT will show the pods running in the 'holmes' namespace. You can verify the configuration by checking the resource limits and ensuring that the Prometheus toolset is enabled.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add holmes https://holmesgpt.dev/helm
helm repo update
helm upgrade holmes holmes/holmes --namespace holmes --set resources.requests.cpu=100m --set resources.requests.memory=2048Mi --set resources.limits.memory=2048Mi
helm upgrade holmes holmes/holmes --namespace holmes --set toolsets.prometheus/metrics.enabled=true

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, and that Helm and kubectl are installed on your local machine.
